entries.definition:
r–q–q ownership, slavery; parchment, scroll, written record; thin, to thin out, to be sheer, to become tattered; to be weak, to be tender; to be clear, to glimmer. Of this root, رَقّ raqq occurs once in the Qur’an.
رَقّ raqq [n.] vellum, parchment, scroll (52:3) فِى رَقٍّ مَّنشُورٍ on a wide-open parchment.
entries.definition_english:
r–q–q ownership, slavery; parchment, scroll, written record; thin, to thin out, to be sheer, to become tattered; to be weak, to be tender; to be clear, to glimmer. Of this root, رَقّ raqq occurs once in the Qur’an.
رَقّ raqq [n.] vellum, parchment, scroll (52:3) فِى رَقٍّ مَّنشُورٍ on a wide-open parchment.
